steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function with slope $6$ has the form
$$
f(x)=6x+b
$$
The graph passes through $(0,4)$, so the $y$-intercept is $4$. That point gives the constant term directly.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: Substitute $b=4$ into the equation: $$ f(x)=6x+4 $$ The answer is C.
